raw_consumer_statement:
I placed an order ( # XXXX ) with XXXX on XX/XX/year>. Ive ordered # XXXX hair bundles from them before, and the XXXX was lighter than what I received this time. The XXXX pack was also much darker than expected, nearly XXXX, even though Ive purchased it locally before and it was lighter. 

Additionally, the packaging had a strong tobacco odor, which was very off-putting. Overall, I was disappointed with the order and contacted them regarding a return. 

I contacted XXXX XXXX times in an attempt to return the unused and unopened hair product. As shown in the attached email correspondence, I first contacted them on XX/XX/year>, the same day I received the shipment. I did not receive a response. 

On XX/XX/year>, I contacted them again through both their website and email. I received a reply stating that they would follow up, but no additional response was ever provided. 

I disputed the charge with my credit card company, Citi. Their response states that I need to work directly with the merchant to resolve the issue. However, I have already contacted the merchant three times in an attempt to do so. Citi has refused to work with me further because I am still in possession of the item. I am willing to return the product, however, without the authorization number and return address, I have no way to do so. 

XXXX requires a Return Merchandise Authorization ( RMA ) before accepting returns. As shown in the ( attached ) screenshot, their policy specifically states that returns will not be accepted unless prior authorization has been issued. 

Because I made every reasonable effort to resolve this matter directly with the merchant and was unable to do so due to their lack of response, I respectfully request that Citi accept this as a valid dispute and issue a full refund.
